mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and improves equity valuation multiples, so sustained rate cuts predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive more often than negative during periods when FEDFUNDS declines for at least 2 consecutive months, or if the sign of the return relationship flips in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS stays elevated, real policy tightens and equity multiples compress, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises by at least 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if inflation surprises above 0.3% do not coincide with weaker forward returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates deteriorating labor demand and lower expected household income, so monthly increases in unemployment predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points from the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day SPX returns are not negative on average after unemployment rises by 0.2 percentage points, or if the trigger fails to separate returns from non-triggered weeks over any 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains in PAYEMS signal stabilizing income and consumption, which supports earnings expectations and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS momentum does not correspond to above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the relationship is absent in more than half of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumption and revenue growth, which should lift equity earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS growth does not predict higher 20-day SPX returns relative to flat or contracting sales periods, or if the sign of the effect turns negative in a rolling 3-year validation.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising while HOUST remains weak, industrial output strength without housing confirmation signals uneven recovery and tighter risk appetite, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive months while HOUST is below its prior 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative 60-day SPX returns on average, or if simultaneous strength in industrial production and housing does not outperform either series alone as a predictor.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST after a prior slump marks a housing-led cyclical upturn that boosts construction activity and consumer confidence, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases by more than 8% month-over-month after at least 3 months of decline
failure_condition: Reject if sharp housing rebounds are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears in subsequent rebounds within a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is slowing while UNRATE is rising, the economy is entering a demand downturn that should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth falls below its prior 4-quarter average and UNRATE rises for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint slowdown signal does not produce negative 60-day SPX returns in a majority of triggered cases, or if GDP slowdown alone explains the effect without unemployment rising.
